# Author:        Behnaz Bagheri
# Last updated:  June 4th 2021
# note: This list is under development
# File with transport data and reaction rates for humid air with 1 percent water: 0.7920N2 0.1980O2 0.01H2O.
# Transport data calculated using Bolsigminus and the Phelps database for N2 and O2 and Itikawa data base for H2O.
# Note that only some of reactions are obtained from Chemical model of Streamer discharges as advancing imperfect conductor, A Luque et al 2017 Plasma Sources Sci. Technol. 26 125006 

reaction_list
-----------------------
# Ionization
e + N2  -> e + e + N2+,field_table,C26 N2 Ionization 15.60 eV
e + N2  -> e + e + N2+,field_table,C27 N2 Ionization 18.80 eV
e + O2  -> e + e + O2+,field_table,C45 O2 Ionization 12.06 eV
#e + H2O -> e + e + H2O+,field_table,C67 H2O Ionization 13.50 eV
#e + H2O -> e + e + H2O+,field_table,C68 H2O Ionization 13.50 eV
#e + H2O -> e + e + H+,field_table,C69 H2O Ionization 16.90 eV
#e + H2O -> e + e + OH+,field_table,C70 H2O Ionization 18.12 eV
#e + H2O -> e + e + O+,field_table,C71 H2O Ionization 19.00 eV
#e + H2O -> e + e + H2+,field_table,C72 H2O Ionization 20.70 eV
#We have the following reaction in Itikawa but strangely, it does not conderve species! where do the two H go?
#e + H2O -> e + e + O++,field_table,C73 H2O Ionization 80.00 eV 
# Attachment
e + O2 + O2 -> O2- + O2,field_table,C29 O2 Attachment
e + O2 -> O + O-,field_table,C30 O2 Attachment
#e + H2O -> H + OH-,field_table,C46 H2O Attachment
#e + H2O -> H2 + O-,field_table,C47 H2O Attachment
#e + H2O -> HO + H-,field_table,C48 H2O Attachment
# Detachment (Panchesnyi 2013)
#O2 + O2- -> e + O2 + O2,exp_v1,1.24e-17 179.0 8.8
#N2 + O2- -> e + O2 + N2,exp_v1,1.24e-17 179.0 8.8
#N2 + O-  -> e + N2O,exp_v1,1.16e-18 48.9 11.0
# Negative ion conversion (Panchesnyi 2013)
#O2  +  O- -> O2- + O,exp_v1,6.96e-17 198.0 5.6
#O2  +  O-  + O2 -> O3- + O2,exp_v2,1.1e-42 65.0
#O2  +  O-  + N2 -> O3- + N2,exp_v2,1.1e-42 65.0
# Negative ion conversion (Gallimberti 1979)
#O2- +  H2O + O2 -> H2OO2- + O2,constant,2.2e-40
#O2- +  H2O + N2 -> H2OO2- + N2,constant,2.2e-40
#H2OO2- + O2   ->  O2- + H2O + O2,k13_func,5.91e-15 1.28e-19 0.18 
#H2OO2- + N2   ->  O2- + H2O + N2,k13_func,5.91e-15 1.28e-19 0.18 
#H2OO2- + H2O + O2 -> H2O_2O2- + O2,constant,5.0e-40
#H2OO2- + H2O + N2 -> H2O_2O2- + N2,constant,5.0e-40
#H2O_2O2- + O2 -> H2OO2- + H2O + O2,k13_func,1.34e-14 5.8e-20 0.18
#H2O_2O2- + N2 -> H2OO2- + H2O + N2,k13_func,1.34e-14 5.8e-20 0.18
#H2O_2O2- + H2O + O2 -> H2O_3O2- + O2,constant,5e-41
#H2O_2O2- + H2O + N2 -> H2O_3O2- + N2,constant,5e-41
#H2O_3O2- + N2 -> H2O_2O2- + H2O + N2,k13_func,1.34e-5 4.49e-20 0.18
#H2O_3O2- + O2 -> H2O_2O2- + H2O + O2,k13_func,1.34e-5 4.49e-20 0.18
# Positive ion conversion (Aleksandrov and Bazelyan 1999)
# These rates depends on the temperature. For T=300K:
#N2+ + N2 + O2 -> N4+ + O2,constant,5.0e-41
#N2+ + N2 + N2 -> N4+ + N2,constant,5.0e-41
#N4+ + O2 -> N2 + N2 + O2+,constant,2.5e-16
#O2+ + O2 + O2 -> O4+ + O2,constant,2.4e-42
#O2+ + O2 + N2 -> O4+ + N2,constant,2.4e-42
# These rates are constant:
#O2+ + H2O + O2 -> H2OO2+ + O2,constant,2.6e-40
#O2+ + H2O + N2 -> H2OO2+ + N2,constant,2.6e-40
#H2OO2+ + H2O -> H3O+ + OH + O2,constant,3e-16
#H3O+ + H2O + N2 -> H2OH3O+ + N2,constant,3e-39 
#H3O+ + H2O + O2 -> H2OH3O+ + O2,constant,3e-39 
#H2OH3O+ + H2O + N2 -> H2O_2H3O+ + N2,constant,3e-39
#H2OH3O+ + H2O + O2 -> H2O_2H3O+ + O2,constant,3e-39
#H2O_2H3O+ + H2O + N2 -> H2O_3H3O+ + N2,constant,3e-39
#H2O_2H3O+ + H2O + O2 -> H2O_3H3O+ + O2,constant,3e-39
# Electron recombination (Kossyi et. al. 1992 cites Smirnov 1983 [in Russian])
#e + O4+ -> O2 + O2,field_table,C00 O4+ Electron Recombination
#e + H2O_3H3O+ -> H + 4H2O,  
---------------------------
